rationale: The cited article is used multiple times in core sections of the paper rather than as a passing mention. The citing study relies on it to define multichannel/dual distribution settings, to justify considering channel variety and intensity jointly, and to inform its operationalization of direct and indirect channel usage. It is also invoked in hypothesis logic about channel conflict and perceived fairness in multichannel relationships. These roles indicate the cited paper materially supports the citing paper's conceptual and measurement foundation.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Sa Vinhas and Anderson (2005) as a key conceptual and empirical anchor for how concurrent direct and indirect channels are defined and analyzed. It then builds its own governance-contingent performance model on top of that grounding.
